notes: v352 — YOUR SHARES ONLY SELL WHEN YOU SAY SO. TRAILING STOP IS NOW OPT-IN. Until now the automatic rules (a trailing stop, break-even and the 10-day max-hold) managed EVERY holding that didn't have a stop you'd set — including shares you bought before you ever set the rules up. That's why removing a stop could sell the share straight away. Fixed: on your 👤 My Trading account, a share is now only ever sold by (a) a 🛑 stop you set, (b) a 🎯 target you set, (c) you selling it, or (d) a 🪤 trailing stop you add to that particular holding. Nothing auto-sells your own shares unless you asked for it, and shares you already hold are left alone. HOW TO ADD A TRAILING STOP: open the ⋯ menu on a holding and choose 🪤 Trailing stop (this share) — pick a percent (e.g. 8%) and it trails that ONE share (the stop only ever rises). The row shows '🪤 trailing 8%'. Remove it any time from the same menu. The 🤖 auto-pilot ($50k practice) account keeps its own rules — that's what it's for. Hard-refresh (Ctrl+F5) to load v352. ALSO RECENT — v351 — a stop you set overrides the rules (🔒) + Undo restored. v350 — table redraw no longer freezes. v349 — data labelled 'end-of-day'. (Older notes live in the Owner's Manual changelog.)
